answer.text: The price paid by the option buyer to the option writer for acquiring the right
The margin deposited by the option writer with the exchange
The intrinsic value of the option at expiry
The brokerage charged by the broker for executing the option trade

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Premium is the PRICE of the option contract. The buyer pays it to the seller (writer) to acquire the right to buy/sell the underlying. It is determined by market forces of supply and demand.
**The Trap:** Premium is NOT margin, not intrinsic value (though intrinsic value is a component), and not brokerage.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Premium = Intrinsic Value + Time Value. This decomposition is critical for pricing questions.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Premium is the cost of the option right. It has two components: intrinsic value and time value.
